description: Fix all confusing sections from Parts 2, 3, 4 of the gap analysis. Improve homepage section clarity, product page hierarchy, checkout flow UX, and admin panel navigation confusion.
steps: Update apps/web/app/page.tsx: Remove 'Most Selling Products' section (duplicate of Weekly Best Selling). Rename 'Just for you' to 'On Sale Today'. Rename 'Today's Fresh Picks' to 'Explore More'. Add brief subtitle/description under each section title explaining why items are grouped. Alternate layouts between horizontal scroll and grid for visual variety.

Update apps/web/app/products/page.tsx: Add a contextual subtitle below 'Browse' heading explaining what the page offers (e.g., 'Find everything you need from our fresh collection'). Add active filter indicators showing which category/sort is active. Add a results count that updates with filters.

Update apps/web/components/checkout-form.tsx: Improve step progression clarity. Add numbered steps or progress indicator (Address > Delivery > Payment > Review). Group related fields visually. Make the order button text more descriptive ('Place Order - Rs X' instead of just 'Place Order').

Update apps/web/components/cart/cart-page-client.tsx: Add empty cart state with illustration and 'Start Shopping' CTA. Add clear item count in header. Show per-item savings inline. Add 'Continue Shopping' button that goes back to products.

Update apps/web/app/dashboard/page.tsx: Improve order status clarity with better visual hierarchy. Add empty state for users with no orders. Make active vs completed orders visually distinct with tabs or sections.

Ensure all customer-facing pages have consistent navigation breadcrumbs or back buttons. Check apps/web/app/account/page.tsx, apps/web/app/checkout/page.tsx, apps/web/app/products/[slug]/page.tsx for navigation clarity.
acceptance_criteria: Homepage has no duplicate product sections
Section titles are descriptive and differentiated
Products page shows contextual info and active filters
Cart has proper empty state and savings info
Dashboard distinguishes active vs completed orders clearly
verification: cd apps/web && npx next build 2>&1 | tail -20
